When we have inbound coming in, we'll just tackle hand raisers, which is the form fields, right?
They say, well, sometimes they can be customers.
Sometimes they could be open opportunities.
Sometimes they're completely disqualified and tire kickers.
And sometimes they're good deals in there.
But looking at statistical trend, 50% of it that comes in, we know are a bunch of customers, live ops, and junk.
The other 50%, we got to work them.
And out of the 50%, about 20% will become something substantial, right?
But that's all tagged and filtered, right?
Yeah.
So then I'm like, who cares then?
Why don't we just automate the whole response?
Because if with our tooling, we can read sentiment, and we can read, when they write us, we can read
Read what they're saying, and we can respond accordingly.
Why not?
The other one is this.
If they have a live op, there's a domain associated.
If it's a customer, there's a tagging, like you say.
Then really, we're not touching those.
Those have to be manual.
Then it's just two buckets of people, the bad leads and the potentially ops that are there.
So if we automate it, I don't think there'll be any harm.
And where do these live right now?
Salesforce, they come in.
Salesforce, and you're looking at Common Room?
We're looking at Common Room to see if we could help address the speed to lead response situation, George.
Because right now, oh my God, you'd be surprised.
The SLA is 24 hours, but then based on some data, it takes the SDRs three days to respond.
I'm like, guys, there's a reason why Safeway discount their cookies significantly on the same day when they don't sell after 2 p.m.
Because nobody wants stale cookies.
I agree with you.
I just sent you the name.
I found it.
Mark Desiani.
Okay, I'm going to go ping him.
Amar, Push told me to connect with you because you got all the secret sauce to all zeros stuff.
But anyway, you know what I'll do?

I'll send in a very concise doc what this XDR AI initiative is, and I wonder how we could even plug in your initial work here to just sort of do like an A-B test on if we just let it run as is and what happens when we automate using this contextual knowledge, how powerful leveraging a smart LLM, like this AI model can be.
we need to even buy a tool like Common Room?
The only nice thing about Common Room is because of the UI, George.
It tells us high intent accounts and high intent leads, right?
But then all this intent is kind of also in the gray area too.
Is that similar to Harmonic?
I'm meeting with Harmonic tomorrow.
Harmonic.
Harmonic is more of like a CRM, and it's spelled a little weird.
I think it's Harmonic, maybe, or Harmonic.ai with a C.
Oh, I see them.
The complete startup database.
Yeah, right now they're, but like if you look in, it's still a pipeline with signals.
And intent that you can track in a bunch.
So it's mostly for like almost all VCs use it, venture capitalists use it, to basically find their next investment.
And it's widely adopted.
So now we're using it as like a personal CRM for our company and tracking clients now because you can get super granular in all the signals that you're tracking that get live updated.
And the outreach workflows and everything are automated.
So I'm doing a demo with them tomorrow.
I can keep you updated on the results and we can like...
Compare notes on what you're hearing from Common Room as well.
Okay, maybe what would be helpful if you don't mind selfishly, can meet with Common Room too and see what you think of them.
The issue is this because you're not even thinking about it.
I don't know if I told you.
I feel like I've been repeating myself, like Groundhog Day kind of thing.
If we think about how Salesforce was created, it is really literally, it's a library for all our customers and prospects, right?
But the way we sell in SaaS has evolved so much that we're hoping that there's a dashboard that's visual, but it doesn't really have that.
So if form is to follow function, that tells me that Salesforce is not that form to support the function that we're looking for to give us the output that we want.
So I don't know if we need another tool, which I'm kind of hesitant to propose because I think what we need whenever we are driving change is three components.
It's culture, people, and process.
And if culturally we are weak, meaning leadership and all that stuff, we can have the best tools and the best process, things will still drop.
Yep.
Anyway, I'm preaching to the choir.
Lots to figure out.
We're excited to help you with it.
Let's do it.
Let's do it.
I have our assignments.
We'll get back to you on progress.
Okay, cool.
Do you want to know what are all the things I'm working on in Q3 that maybe we can start working on those as well?
Yeah, to get ahead of them?
Yeah.
Yeah, yeah, because I've got B2B, SaaS, financial services, and they're different too, right?
Because we have corporate, commercial space, and enterprise.
Okay, let's – I mean, we can go crazy with it, but let's start with retail, right?
And then knowing these, we can start building specific artifacts for those as well.
But I really want to knock retail out of the park and get that process down and, like, implemented.
And then –
You have one, like the outcome and the success from it to then.
Build on it.
Yep.
Okay.
Sounds good.
Let me know what you need and then I'll send over some stuff on the XDR AI initiative and maybe we can figure out how this can plug into that.
Amazing.
Yep.
I'm also going to be looping Kyle in.
He's going to be helping on this account because I am not changing roles, but I'm going to be the chief of staff now here.
So still helping on clients, but also taking like a higher level process and trying to fix a bunch more things.
So I'll still be here and Kyle's amazing.
So you'll just be seeing him more as well.
All right.
Thank you, George.
Thanks, Rachel.
This was great.
We'll talk soon.
Yep.
Talk soon.
Bye-bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly sync between Okta and growthx.ai to discuss progress on retail-focused product personas and explore potential AI initiatives for Okta's sales processes.](https://fathom.video/share/Fqmw-uTxJd_87Nc6isSVVDY-q4eCEXed?tab=summary&timestamp=60.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Rachael provided feedback on growthx.ai's initial retail persona documents, emphasizing the importance of nuanced language and specificity](https://fathom.video/share/Fqmw-uTxJd_87Nc6isSVVDY-q4eCEXed?tab=summary&timestamp=204.0)
  - [Okta is exploring AI tools like Common Room to improve inbound lead response times and automate parts of the SDR workflow](https://fathom.video/share/Fqmw-uTxJd_87Nc6isSVVDY-q4eCEXed?tab=summary&timestamp=1129.0)
  - [growthx.ai is refining their process for creating tailored company/persona briefs to enable more effective sales conversations](https://fathom.video/share/Fqmw-uTxJd_87Nc6isSVVDY-q4eCEXed?tab=summary&timestamp=694.0)
  - [Both parties agreed to focus on perfecting the retail vertical approach before expanding to other industries](https://fathom.video/share/Fqmw-uTxJd_87Nc6isSVVDY-q4eCEXed?tab=summary&timestamp=579.0)

## Topics

### Feedback on Retail Persona Documents

  - [Rachael appreciated the level of detail but suggested using more industry-specific language (e.g. "dev teams" instead of "engineering teams")](https://fathom.video/share/Fqmw-uTxJd_87Nc6isSVVDY-q4eCEXed?tab=summary&timestamp=320.0)
  - [She emphasized the importance of vertical-specific context in setting the tone for sales conversations](https://fathom.video/share/Fqmw-uTxJd_87Nc6isSVVDY-q4eCEXed?tab=summary&timestamp=458.0)
  - [Rachael highlighted the "how to win their trust" and "how to annoy them" sections as particularly useful](https://fathom.video/share/Fqmw-uTxJd_87Nc6isSVVDY-q4eCEXed?tab=summary&timestamp=632.0)

### Okta's AI Initiative for Sales

  - [Okta is evaluating tools like Common Room to improve inbound lead response times](https://fathom.video/share/Fqmw-uTxJd_87Nc6isSVVDY-q4eCEXed?tab=summary&timestamp=1129.0)
  - [Current SLA is 24 hours, but actual response time averages 3 days](https://fathom.video/share/Fqmw-uTxJd_87Nc6isSVVDY-q4eCEXed?tab=summary&timestamp=1143.0)
  - [Rachael proposes automating responses for certain lead categories (e.g. existing customers, disqualified leads)](https://fathom.video/share/Fqmw-uTxJd_87Nc6isSVVDY-q4eCEXed?tab=summary&timestamp=2090.0)
  - [Goal is to free up SDRs to focus on higher-value activities like understanding buyer needs and deal strategy](https://fathom.video/share/Fqmw-uTxJd_87Nc6isSVVDY-q4eCEXed?tab=summary&timestamp=680.0)

### growthx.ai's Approach to Persona/Company Briefs

  - [Creating detailed artifacts for company context, audience personas, and specific verticals](https://fathom.video/share/Fqmw-uTxJd_87Nc6isSVVDY-q4eCEXed?tab=summary&timestamp=186.0)
  - [Aim to combine these into highly tailored briefs for individual prospects](https://fathom.video/share/Fqmw-uTxJd_87Nc6isSVVDY-q4eCEXed?tab=summary&timestamp=706.0)
  - [George is working on refining the process for real-time brief creation based on incoming prospect data](https://fathom.video/share/Fqmw-uTxJd_87Nc6isSVVDY-q4eCEXed?tab=summary&timestamp=694.0)

### Evolving Role of SDRs

  - [Rachael believes SDRs should move beyond just booking meetings to taking on early-stage opportunity qualification](https://fathom.video/share/Fqmw-uTxJd_87Nc6isSVVDY-q4eCEXed?tab=summary&timestamp=1981.0)
  - [This includes understanding buying processes, stakeholders, and deal strategy before passing to AEs](https://fathom.video/share/Fqmw-uTxJd_87Nc6isSVVDY-q4eCEXed?tab=summary&timestamp=1988.0)

### Upcoming Meetings and Connections

  - [growthx.ai team to meet with Brad and Tiff for further retail insights](https://fathom.video/share/Fqmw-uTxJd_87Nc6isSVVDY-q4eCEXed?tab=summary&timestamp=1099.0)
  - [Rachael to connect George with Mark Desiani and Sam (last name TBD) for additional Auth0 product knowledge](https://fathom.video/share/Fqmw-uTxJd_87Nc6isSVVDY-q4eCEXed?tab=summary&timestamp=1123.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Rachael to find and connect George with Mark Desiani for more granular Auth0 product insights](https://fathom.video/share/Fqmw-uTxJd_87Nc6isSVVDY-q4eCEXed?tab=summary&timestamp=1139.0)
  - [Rachael to send document outlining Okta's XDR AI initiative for potential integration with growthx.ai's work](https://fathom.video/share/Fqmw-uTxJd_87Nc6isSVVDY-q4eCEXed?tab=summary&timestamp=2180.0)
  - [George to share insights from upcoming demo with Harmonic.ai (potential CRM alternative)](https://fathom.video/share/Fqmw-uTxJd_87Nc6isSVVDY-q4eCEXed?tab=summary&timestamp=2227.0)
  - [Team to focus on perfecting retail vertical approach before expanding to other industries (B2B SaaS, financial services)](https://fathom.video/share/Fqmw-uTxJd_87Nc6isSVVDY-q4eCEXed?tab=summary&timestamp=2380.0)
  - [Kyle from growthx.ai to take on more active role in account management as George transitions to Chief of Staff](https://fathom.video/share/Fqmw-uTxJd_87Nc6isSVVDY-q4eCEXed?tab=summary&timestamp=2421.0)

action_items.description: Create credit union-specific artifact after Sam intro
Intro George to Sam (SE/TE) for credit union-specific artifact insights
Ping Mark Desiani re Auth0 secret sauce
Send concise doc on XDR AI initiative to George
Loop in Kyle to help on Okta account
